assertions.text: Character count is shown in parentheses after each headline
Each variant serves a genuinely distinct purpose (news, SEO, engagement, feature, social), not minor word swaps
The most editorially rigorous headline is listed first
Every headline accurately reflects the story without overstating or fabricating claims
Each lede is labeled with its type (e.g., Summary lede - hard news, Scene-setting lede - feature)
Word count is shown in parentheses after each lede
No lede opens with filler phrases like 'In today's world', 'It's no secret', 'Now more than ever', or 'In recent years'
Active voice is used throughout except where journalistically necessary
The key fact or hook appears in the first sentence, not buried in sentence two or three
